reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal Putnam 1962 A6 problem. The closure conditions `hSadd` and `hSprod` directly match "a+b ∈ S" and "ab ∈ S" whenever a, b ∈ S. The "exactly one of three" condition is correctly encoded as the conjunction of "at least one" (r ∈ S ∨ -r ∈ S ∨ r = 0) and three pairwise mutual-exclusion clauses (¬(r ∈ S ∧ -r ∈ S), ¬(r ∈ S ∧ r = 0), ¬(-r ∈ S ∧ r = 0)), which is logically equivalent to "exactly one of the three holds". The conclusion `S = { r : ℚ | r > 0 }` correctly represents "S is the set of all positive rational numbers". No hypotheses are added, omitted, or modified, and no types are changed.
